description: Add a DIMACS benchmark that reads instances from a directory specified via env var (no fixtures committed).
commit: test: add DIMACS benchmark skeleton reading from env-var path
steps: Define `var dimacsInstances = []struct{ Name string; KnownOptimum int64 }{{"netgen_8_10a.min", 0}, {"gridgen_sr_8a.min", 0}}`. KnownOptimum=0 means 'not known; smoke test only' — do NOT fabricate values; a downstream operator fills these in from Kovacs 2015.
`func dimacsDir(tb testing.TB) string`: read os.Getenv("GO_MCF_DIMACS_DIR"); if empty call tb.Skip with a message including the LEMON wiki URL.

`func BenchmarkDIMACS(b *testing.B)`: call dimacsDir(b). For each entry run `b.Run(name, ...)`: open the file under `filepath.Join(dir, name)` (missing file → b.Skipf), ParseDIMACS once outside the timed loop, b.ResetTimer, then for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ call Solve.

`func TestDIMACSCorrectness(t *testing.T)`: call dimacsDir(t). For each entry with KnownOptimum != 0 assert Result.TotalCost == KnownOptimum; otherwise (KnownOptimum==0) smoke-test: assert err == nil and Result.Feasible == true.
Verify without GO_MCF_DIMACS_DIR set: `go test ./...` passes with skip lines; `go test -bench=. -benchtime=1x ./...` exits 0.
acceptance_criteria: Both skip cleanly when GO_MCF_DIMACS_DIR is unset.
No DIMACS fixture files are committed.
dimacsInstances entries use KnownOptimum=0 for unknown optima (no fabricated values).
`go test ./...` passes (with skip lines when env var unset).
`go test -bench=. -benchtime=1x ./...` exits 0 with skip messages when env var unset.
